metadata.name: Regain Superuser Access and Reset the Root Password
metadata.description: Gain administrative access to a system when the superuser password is unknown or is locked
prerequisites: Understanding of the Linux boot process

hosts.name: servera
hosts.description: Target server requiring root password reset

tasks.name: Verify root password hash exists
tasks.description: The root user should have a valid password hash in /etc/shadow

checks.description: Check root has a password hash in /etc/shadow

tasks.failure_message: Root password hash not found in /etc/shadow
hints: Boot with init=/bin/bash appended to kernel command line
Remount root filesystem read-write: mount -o remount,rw /
Use passwd command to set password to 'redhat'

tasks.name: Verify /etc/shadow file exists
tasks.description: The /etc/shadow file should exist and contain root password hash

checks.description: Check root has a valid password hash (not locked)

tasks.success_message: /etc/shadow exists with valid root password hash
tasks.failure_message: /etc/shadow is missing or root account is locked
hints: The /etc/shadow file should contain password hashes
Root's password field should not start with ! or *

tasks.name: Verify /etc/shadow has correct SELinux context
tasks.description: The /etc/shadow file should have the proper SELinux context after relabeling
hints: Create /.autorelabel file before rebooting to trigger SELinux relabeling

tasks.name: Verify autorelabel file is removed after reboot
tasks.description: The /.autorelabel file should be removed after successful SELinux relabeling
tasks.failure_message: /.autorelabel file still exists - system may not have rebooted after relabeling
hints: The /.autorelabel file is automatically removed after relabeling completes
If it still exists, the system may need to reboot again

tasks.name: Verify root account is not locked
tasks.description: The root account should not be locked in /etc/shadow

checks.description: Check root account password status is set (not locked)

tasks.success_message: Root account is unlocked and has a password set
tasks.failure_message: Root account appears to be locked
hints: Use 'passwd -S root' to check account status
PS or P means password is set, LK means locked
Use 'passwd root' to set a new password
